picks.title: Maker Market
picks.blurb: Weekend market of handmade creative products: jewellery, art, design. Covered venue with live music and artisan goods.

picks.title: Beyond the Manosphere – Masculinities Today
picks.blurb: Ambitious group show examining contemporary masculinities and power. Final weeks before August 2 closure.

picks.title: Het nieuwe ARTIS-Aquarium
picks.blurb: Brand-new aquarium at ARTIS with water-themed activities and exhibitions throughout summer.
picks.why: New attraction; summer activities center on water

picks.title: Bar Dancing Multipla Closing Weekend
picks.blurb: Iconic garage venue's final hurrah. Orange-lit dancefloor, fogged-up doors, DJ lineup spanning techno to house. Last weekend ever.

picks.title: Lemon
picks.blurb: Live performance at iconic Amsterdam hotel venue on Sunday.

picks.title: Escape Amsterdam
picks.blurb: Late-night house and techno club with rotating DJs, deLux room, and extended after-hours sets both nights.

picks.title: Pure Markt
picks.blurb: 60+ artisanal vendors: organic produce, handmade lifestyle goods, art, local food stalls. Sunny Sunday vibe.
picks.why: Quality Sunday market with organic & local focus

picks.title: Kho Liang Ie – Mid-Century Modernist
picks.blurb: Landmark survey of the Dutch-Indonesian designer's furniture, architecture, and visionary interiors.

picks.title: Houthavens Eats
picks.blurb: Waterfront food event at Houthavens with food vendors and drinks by the IJ river.

picks.title: Watercolor Painting Workshop – Canal Houses
picks.blurb: Paint Amsterdam's iconic canal houses in watercolor. Hands-on guided session creating still-life artwork inspired by local architecture.
picks.why: Creative weekend art class, confirmed June 28

picks.title: The Great Recycle Summer Festival
picks.blurb: Free sustainability festival for kids and families. Recycling workshops, creative activities, Recycle Rally finale.

picks.title: Eye Classics
picks.blurb: Curated film history on the big screen, screened in 35mm whenever possible. Explore cinema's evolution from early beginnings to today.

picks.title: Vondelpark Open Air Theatre
picks.blurb: Free summer performances: this weekend Jungle by Night, Vokall with Alain Clark, and Isabel van Gelder.

picks.title: Amsterdam Roots Festival
picks.blurb: Free world music, circus, dance & theatre festival. 44th edition with artists from across the globe—multicultural, grassroots vibes.
picks.why: free, diverse world music & culture celebration
picks.source: Amsterdam Roots Festival · Amsterdam Roots Festival / I amsterdam · I amsterdam · Rick Steves / Amsterdam Bike Taxi · Your Little Black Book / I amsterdam · I amsterdam / Your Little Black Book · AmsterdamTips & I amsterdam

picks.title: Holland Festival
picks.blurb: Amsterdam's premier international arts festival wraps this weekend with theatre, dance, and music performances across multiple venues.
picks.why: Final weekend of major cultural event
picks.source: I amsterdam · Outhere / Holland Festival · Holland Festival / Muziekgebouw

picks.title: La Pegatina
picks.blurb: Barcelona street-turned-arena act blends rumba catalana, ska, punk, and cumbia. Over 1200 concerts deep—return tour after year out.
picks.why: high-energy Latin fusion returns to Amsterdam

picks.title: The Maker Market
picks.blurb: Weekend makers market: handmade jewelry, art, design. Live music and creative community in covered passage.

picks.title: Wallen Festival
picks.blurb: Fourth edition of neighbourhood festival with 90+ locations. Walks, live music, local food, workshops across Amsterdam's historic centre.
picks.why: 90+ venues open both days, rarely available
picks.source: Gemeente Amsterdam · Your Little Black Book

picks.title: IJ-Hallen Flea Market
picks.blurb: Large weekend flea market with 750 outdoor stands—vintage, design, collectibles. Early start beats crowds.

picks.title: Food for Thought Festival
picks.blurb: Caribbean soul food, reggae, rap, poetry. Free festival honouring Keti Koti (slavery abolition), music and performances.
picks.why: Cultural celebration, free food and music
picks.source: Food For Thought / Amsterdam Spotted · Amsterdam Spotted

picks.title: The Movies — arthouse cinema
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ oldest cinema still running — Art Deco rooms, a dinner-and-a-film deal, arthouse bills.
picks.why: Indoor · dinner + a film · perfect when it’s wet

picks.title: East Beach — rooftop city beach
picks.blurb: A new 800m² rooftop city beach above Oosterpark — sand, beach chairs, drinks, open to all.

picks.title: Carel Visser — sculpture in the gardens
picks.blurb: Free outdoor sculpture show — the first artist solo in the Rijksmuseum gardens, opening this weekend.

picks.title: De Pimpelmees
picks.blurb: Cosy neighbourhood wine bar — European wines from €6, Italian snacks, free chips. Opened early 2026.

picks.title: Multipla Club Closing
picks.blurb: 48-hour closing weekend with eclectic club sounds, community-curated sets, raw garage feel—the final dance.

picks.title: Spinoza — De Mokum Musical
picks.blurb: Open-air musical about philosopher Spinoza set in old Amsterdam, in the forest theatre.
picks.why: Cross-source (LBB + I amsterdam) · open-air · dry-night plan

picks.title: IJ-Hallen
picks.blurb: Europe's largest flea market with 750 outdoor stalls selling vintage, secondhand goods, clothes, curiosities.

picks.title: Keti Koti: Naar Ndakaaru zwemmen
picks.blurb: Celebrate and commemorate Keti Koti with stories, conversations, food, and music for families.
picks.why: Cultural celebration with family activities and swimming

picks.title: Queer Power: World Pride celebration
picks.blurb: Eye's festival celebrating queer cinema from around the world, timed with Amsterdam's World Pride. Films, talks, and events exploring freedom and diversity.
picks.why: World Pride weekend; major cultural celebration

picks.title: Sunday Clay Club – Ceramics Workshop
picks.blurb: Relaxed intro to clay work for beginners. Hand-building and wheel throwing in three hours, make your own mug or bowl.
picks.why: Only confirmed workshop on June 28 weekend

picks.title: WOPL! Zomerfeest
picks.blurb: Family-friendly afternoon celebrating summer with games, music, and fun for all ages.
picks.why: Perfect weekend family activity with food and music

picks.title: As
picks.blurb: Wood-fired, ingredient-led cooking and natural wine in a striking round garden pavilion.

picks.title: BAK
picks.blurb: A light-filled warehouse with an open kitchen and an art-covered wall; a set seasonal menu of produce-driven dishes.

picks.title: Eye(s) Open: Colonial Film Heritage
picks.blurb: Eleven contemporary artists respond to Eye's archive of colonial-era films from Indonesia and Suriname with ten new commissioned works.
picks.why: Bold reckoning with film history; new artist commissions

picks.title: Foam
picks.blurb: A nimble photography museum in a canal house — big-name retrospectives and emerging talent, always worth the drop-in.

picks.title: Choux
picks.blurb: Inventive, veg-forward small plates and natural wine in a raw red-brick space near the IJ.

picks.title: Moise
picks.blurb: An understated De Pijp boutique with a razor-sharp eye — Acne, Lemaire, Marant and the like, hand-picked and beautifully shown.

picks.title: Patta
picks.blurb: The homegrown streetwear label and culture hub — drops, sneakers and the community that put Amsterdam on the map.

picks.title: Pllek — waterfront hang
picks.blurb: A cargo-container café-bar on the NDSM wharf — big windows onto the IJ, sofas, and a wood stove when it turns.
picks.why: Indoor seating · IJ views · cozy when grey

picks.title: RIJKS®
picks.blurb: Joris Bijdendijk’s Michelin-starred dining room beside the Rijksmuseum — the “cuisine of the Low Countries”, done with flair.

picks.title: X Bank
picks.blurb: A concept store in a former bank showing 180+ Dutch fashion, design and art labels — couture to hand-thrown ceramics.
picks.why: Best of Dutch design · one room

picks.title: Rijsel
picks.blurb: Lille-inspired French cooking in a converted school — that rotisserie chicken is the order.

picks.title: Bar Fisk
picks.blurb: Sun-soaked Mediterranean seafood and sharing plates in De Pijp.

picks.title: Haarlem — the easy escape
picks.blurb: A calmer historic city 15 min away — Grote Markt, the St-Bavo organ, a Saturday market and café-lined square.

picks.title: Mobilia
picks.blurb: Three floors of high design on the Utrechtsestraat — icons by the masters alongside more attainable covetables.

picks.title: Vlieger
picks.blurb: A two-floor temple to paper since 1869 — hundreds of colours, handmade sheets and card stacked to the ceiling.

picks.title: Café Chris — oldest brown café
picks.blurb: Open since 1624: a creaky, golden-lit Jordaan brown café for a slow afternoon beer while it pours outside.

picks.title: De Ceuvel
picks.blurb: A café on a reclaimed shipyard — a circular-economy experiment with a ramshackle, plant-filled terrace.

picks.title: Efteling
picks.blurb: Europe’s oldest fairytale theme park — enchanted forest, dark rides and proper coasters in a storybook world.

picks.title: Mata Hari
picks.blurb: A warm canal-side bar-restaurant on the Wallen — candlelit, characterful and a local antidote to the crowds.

picks.title: Micropia
picks.blurb: The world’s only microbe museum — microscopes, a kiss-o-meter and the tiny life all around us.

picks.title: Skatecafé
picks.blurb: A working skate ramp inside a former garage, with a kitchen, bar and DJs — as fun as it sounds.
picks.why: A skate ramp in a restaurant

picks.title: Café de Tuin
picks.blurb: A quintessential Jordaan brown café — newspapers, good beer and an easy neighbourhood buzz.

picks.title: Kriterion
picks.blurb: A legendary student-run cinema and café since 1945 — arthouse programming with a buzzy terrace.

picks.title: Madurodam
picks.blurb: The whole of the Netherlands at 1:25 — miniature canals, Schiphol and windmills kids can tower over.

picks.title: Sauna Deco — Art Deco baths
picks.blurb: A 1920s Art Deco bathhouse (salvaged from a Paris department store) with saunas, steam and a quiet tea room.
picks.why: Warm · restorative · the ultimate cold-and-wet antidote

picks.title: Friday Next
picks.blurb: A concept store and café in one — young Dutch design to browse, with brunch and coffee while you do it.

picks.title: TonTon Club
picks.blurb: An arcade bar where you pay to play — pinball, classic cabinets and Japanese games, with beer and gyoza on tap.

picks.title: WonderWood
picks.blurb: A gallery-shop devoted to plywood and mid-century modern — bentwood chairs and design classics since 1999.

picks.title: Rijksmuseum
picks.blurb: The Netherlands’ treasure house — Rembrandt’s Night Watch, Vermeer and the Golden Age masters in a cathedral of a building.
picks.why: The Night Watch · the big one

picks.title: De Filmhallen
picks.blurb: A nine-screen arthouse cinema in a converted tram depot — with a bustling food hall right next door.
picks.why: Arthouse · in the old tram depot

picks.title: ARTIS Royal Zoo
picks.blurb: The country’s oldest zoo — big cats, an aquarium and a planetarium across a leafy 19th-century park.
picks.why: All-day with kids · indoor + outdoor mix

picks.title: Eye Filmmuseum — repertory screening
picks.blurb: Across the IJ by free ferry: a striking film museum with a daily repertory programme and a river-view café.
picks.why: Indoor · a film + the ferry ride · weather-proof

picks.title: Cowboys to Catwalk
picks.blurb: A treasure-trove of vintage designer — Margiela, Marc Jacobs and one-off finds rummaged from decades of fashion.

picks.title: Zaanse Schans windmills
picks.blurb: Eight working windmills, cheese and crafts on the Zaan — free grounds, ~17 min train from Centraal.
picks.why: Short train · outdoor · classic kid day
